name: APT28
description: Russian GRU Unit 26165 threat group active since at least 2004. Primarily targets governments, militaries, and security organizations.

name: APT29
description: Russian SVR threat group responsible for the SolarWinds supply chain attack and numerous government breaches. Known for stealthy, long-dwell intrusions.

name: Sandworm
description: Russian GRU Unit 74455 group responsible for NotPetya, attacks on Ukrainian power grid, and Olympic Destroyer. Focuses on destructive operations.

name: Turla
description: Russian FSB-linked group known for sophisticated backdoors and hijacking other threat actors' infrastructure. Active since at least 2004.

name: Lazarus Group
description: North Korean state-sponsored group linked to the $81M Bangladesh Bank heist, WannaCry ransomware, and Sony Pictures breach. The most prolific nation-state financial threat actor.

name: APT41
description: Chinese group uniquely conducting both state-sponsored espionage and financially motivated cybercrime. Notable for supply chain attacks and video game industry targeting.

name: APT17
description: Chinese threat group that has targeted U.S. government entities, the defense industry, law firms, and IT companies.

name: APT10
description: Chinese group primarily targeting managed service providers to indirectly reach their clients. Known for Operation Cloud Hopper.

name: APT32
description: Vietnamese state-sponsored group targeting foreign corporations, governments, and journalists with access to political, economic, and corporate secrets.

name: OilRig
description: Iranian government-linked group targeting Middle Eastern and international organizations in energy, financial, government, and critical infrastructure sectors.

name: APT33
description: Iranian group targeting aerospace, energy, and petrochemical industries. Associated with destructive Shamoon malware variants and password spraying campaigns.

name: FIN7
description: Sophisticated financially motivated group targeting the hospitality, restaurant, and retail industries. Known for spear-phishing with malicious documents and point-of-sale malware.

name: Carbanak
description: Cybercriminal group responsible for stealing over $1 billion from banks worldwide. Uses spear-phishing to gain access to banking networks.

name: Wizard Spider
description: Russia-based cybercriminal group operating TrickBot, Ryuk, Conti, and BazarLoader. One of the most prolific ransomware operators targeting hospitals and critical infrastructure.

name: TA505
description: Prolific cybercriminal group distributing Locky, Dridex, FlawedAmmyy, and Clop ransomware. Among the highest-volume email threat actors globally.

name: Cobalt Group
description: Cybercriminal group targeting banks and ATM networks, primarily in Eastern Europe and Central Asia. Known for using Cobalt Strike and custom ATM malware.

name: FIN6
description: Financially motivated group primarily targeting payment card data from point-of-sale systems in the retail, hospitality, and restaurant industries.

name: LAPSUS$
description: Loosely organized group known for social engineering, SIM swapping, and extortion. Breached Microsoft, Okta, Samsung, Nvidia, and Uber through credential theft and insider recruitment.

name: TEMP.Veles
description: Group responsible for the Triton/TRISIS attack targeting Schneider Electric safety systems at a Saudi petrochemical plant — the first known malware designed to cause physical damage.

name: APT38
description: North Korean group focused solely on financial gain, responsible for SWIFT banking heists totaling over $2 billion. Distinct from Lazarus Group though shares infrastructure.

name: RTM
description: Cybercriminal group targeting Russian businesses with banking trojans since 2015. Uses phishing emails with malicious attachments to steal online banking credentials.

name: SilverTerrier
description: Nigerian cybercriminal group conducting business email compromise (BEC) fraud and commodity malware campaigns targeting global organizations.

name: Suckfly
description: Chinese espionage group known for stealing code signing certificates to sign malware, targeting Indian government and commercial organizations.

name: FIN4
description: Group uniquely targeting healthcare and pharmaceutical industries for material non-public information for stock trading purposes.

name: BRONZE BUTLER
description: Chinese group primarily targeting Japanese organizations in critical infrastructure, heavy industry, and manufacturing sectors.
